description_long: This object consists of four swords of varying lengths, each featuring a straight, double-edged blade and a distinct crossguard. The swords range from a large two-handed longsword with an extended crossguard and round pommel to shorter, more compact blades with simpler hilts. The blades are metallic and appear to be forged from steel, with a dark, matte finish that suggests a utilitarian or battle-worn appearance. The handles are cylindrical and capped with rounded or slightly angular pommels, and the crossguards differ in style, with some being straight and others slightly curved or flared. There is no visible ornamentation or decorative detailing, emphasizing function over form. The overall design is reminiscent of medieval European swords, suitable for display, collection, or theatrical use in an urban context.
description: A set of four straight-bladed, double-edged swords with varying lengths and simple, functional hilts. The swords have dark metallic blades and distinct crossguards, lacking any visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Four swords are shown upright, highlighting the differences in crossguard shapes and pommel designs.
description_view_1: The swords are viewed from the side, clearly displaying the varying blade lengths and the profile of each crossguard.
description_view_2: The swords are seen from the back, emphasizing the handles and the symmetry of the blades.
description_view_3: The swords are shown from the opposite side, again revealing blade length differences and the silhouette of the hilts.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, dark, simple, with rounded or angular pommels
